name: Question Discipline
shortDescription: Finds the one question that changes the most downstream decisions.
problem: Most question lists optimize for coverage. This skill optimizes for leverage.
howItWorks: It names the decision, identifies the eigenquestion, ranks supporting questions, cuts low-signal asks, and rewrites the final questions in a direct operator voice.
tools: Connected apps when context is needed
buildNotes: Built around a repeated pattern in Corp Dev, advisory, interview, and meeting prep work: fewer questions, sharper signal.
portfolioCopy: A question strategy agent for moments where the question itself is the work.

name: But For Real
shortDescription: Runs an adversarial second-pass review before important work ships.
problem: AI first drafts often sound complete before they are true, useful, or safe to rely on.
howItWorks: It checks assumptions, user intent, execution path, quality bar, verification proof, and remaining limits before finalizing meaningful work.
tools: Tests or validation scripts when relevant
Source checks when facts are date-sensitive
buildNotes: Built as a critic layer for an AI-native operating system where generation is cheap but judgment still matters.
portfolioCopy: A final-review agent that catches the clean-sounding answer before it creates rework.

name: Skill Editor
shortDescription: Audits and improves agent skills so they trigger cleanly and stay useful.
problem: Local skills decay. Trigger language gets vague, instructions duplicate, evals miss the real failure modes, and AI slop creeps in.
howItWorks: It reviews trigger quality, removes stale or generic instructions, tightens the workflow, updates evals, and preserves the judgment that makes the skill useful.
buildNotes: Built after skill creation became a repeatable workflow and needed its own maintenance layer.
portfolioCopy: A skill-maintenance agent for keeping an agent system sharp as it grows.

name: HTML Slides
shortDescription: Builds polished single-file HTML decks for executive, advisory, and async review work.
problem: Slide work often gets trapped between generic templates and brittle exported screenshots.
howItWorks: It classifies the deck mode, loads the right source context, generates title-slide previews when style is open, builds a fixed-stage native HTML deck, and runs visual QA.
tools: PPTX extraction when templates are provided
buildNotes: Built for Jann's executive and advisory workflows where decks need to be readable, specific, and polished without becoming design theater.
portfolioCopy: A deck-building agent that treats slides as decision artifacts, not decorative output.

name: Meeting Coach
shortDescription: Reviews meeting transcripts and gives blunt, specific coaching on executive communication.
problem: Generic meeting feedback is useless. The leverage is in transcript-specific moments: where the point was buried, the question was soft, or the decision moment was missed.
howItWorks: It identifies the speaker, reviews transcript moments through a communication scorecard, rewrites high-leverage turns, and gives three concrete reps for the next meeting.
tools: Granola or meeting notes when available
buildNotes: Built to turn real meeting transcripts into a practical coaching loop for concision, persuasion, question quality, and decision-driving.
portfolioCopy: A meeting-coaching agent that turns transcripts into behavior you can practice next time.
